name: Shortcut (lnk) File Executing Commands (Windows)
notes: This rule triggers when a shortcut (lnk) file has created processes that can execute commands. Several malware, such as Emotet,  use shortcut file that when opened execute malicious commands.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Shortcut (lnk) File Executing a Critical Process (1)
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Shortcut (lnk) File Executing a Critical Process (2)
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Shortcut (lnk) File Executing a Critical Process (1), BB:BehaviorDefinition: Shortcut (lnk) File Executing a Critical Process (2)
TD_list: APPLY Shortcut (lnk) File Executing Commands (Windows) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Shortcut (lnk) File Executing a Critical Process (1), BB:BehaviorDefinition: Shortcut (lnk) File Executing a Critical Process (2)
RED_list: Event name: Shortcut (lnk) File Executing Commands (Windows)
Event description: Detects when a shortcut (lnk) file has created processes that can execute commands.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
